name: specgofer
description: Spec-driven development for AI. Exposes 6 MCP tools that let Claude Code and GitHub Copilot autonomously implement features from GitHub Spec Kit specifications.

commands.title: SpecGofer: Upgrade to Spec Kit Format
SpecGofer: Execute All Pending Specs (Dependency Order)
SpecGofer: Pause Claude Code Terminal (Send ESC)
SpecGofer: Resume Claude Code Autonomous Monitoring

viewsWelcome.contents:
No SpecGofer structure found in this workspace.
[Initialize SpecGofer](command:specGofer.initialize)
This will create the .specify folder structure following GitHub Spec Kit standards.

specGofer.anthropicApiKey.description: Anthropic API Key for Claude (get yours at https://console.anthropic.com/settings/keys)
specGofer.anthropicApiKey.markdownDescription: Your Anthropic API key for Claude. [Get one here](https://console.anthropic.com/settings/keys). This is stored securely in VSCode settings and never shared.

specGofer.googleApiKey.description: Google AI API Key for Gemini (get yours at https://aistudio.google.com/apikey)
specGofer.googleApiKey.markdownDescription: Your Google AI API key for Gemini models. [Get one here](https://aistudio.google.com/apikey). Used by LLM Council feature for multi-provider execution.

specGofer.openaiApiKey.description: OpenAI API Key for GPT models (get yours at https://platform.openai.com/api-keys)
specGofer.openaiApiKey.markdownDescription: Your OpenAI API key for GPT models. [Get one here](https://platform.openai.com/api-keys). Used by LLM Council feature for multi-provider execution.

specGofer.autonomousMode.description: Enable autonomous question answering in Claude Code terminal
specGofer.autonomousMode.markdownDescription: When enabled, SpecGofer will automatically answer Claude Code's questions using Claude 3.5 Haiku with full context (constitution, spec, tasks). Requires `specGofer.anthropicApiKey` to be set.

specGofer.autoInitialize.description: Automatically offer to initialize SpecGofer when opening a repository without it

specGofer.preferredAI.description: Preferred AI tool when sending tasks (ask, claude, copilot)

specGofer.claudeTerminalName.description: Name pattern to identify Claude Code terminals

specGofer.autoValidate.description: Automatically validate specs against constitution when creating/updating

specGofer.showWelcome.description: Show welcome message and quick start when initializing

enumDescriptions: Mark Sharp - Fast WYSIWYG editor with live preview (requires extension: JonathanYeung.mark-sharp)
Markdown Editor - Feature-rich WYSIWYG editor (requires extension: zaaack.markdown-editor)
Markdown WYSIWYG - Simple WYSIWYG editor (requires extension: adamerose.markdown-wysiwyg)
specGofer.markdownViewer.description: Choose how to view markdown files when clicking tree view items
specGofer.markdownViewer.markdownDescription:
Choose how to view markdown files when clicking tree view items.

**Recommended WYSIWYG Extensions:**
- [Mark Sharp](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=JonathanYeung.mark-sharp) - Fast & modern
- [Markdown Editor](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=zaaack.markdown-editor) - Feature-rich
- [Markdown WYSIWYG](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=adamerose.markdown-wysiwyg) - Simple

specGofer.autonomous.showTerminals.description: Show Claude Code terminals during autonomous execution
specGofer.autonomous.showTerminals.markdownDescription:
Whether to show Claude Code terminals when SpecGofer runs autonomous implementation.

- `true`: Terminals are visible (you can watch progress)
- `false`: Terminals are hidden (cleaner UI)

enumDescriptions: Send notifications via WhatsApp (requires phone number)
Send notifications via email (requires email address)
specGofer.autonomous.notificationChannel.description: How to receive notifications from autonomous execution
specGofer.autonomous.notificationChannel.markdownDescription:
Choose how you want to receive notifications when SpecGofer needs your attention during autonomous execution.

- `vscode`: Notification popups in VSCode (default)
- `whatsapp`: Send messages to your phone (requires WhatsApp setup)
- `email`: Send email notifications (requires email setup)

specGofer.autonomous.whatsappPhoneNumber.markdownDescription:
Your WhatsApp phone number for receiving notifications.

**Format**: Include country code with +, e.g., `+1234567890`

**Note**: Requires WhatsApp to be set up and running.

specGofer.autonomous.emailAddress.markdownDescription: Your email address for receiving notifications from autonomous execution.

specGofer.autonomous.maxRetries.description: Maximum number of retry attempts for errors (1-5)
specGofer.autonomous.maxRetries.markdownDescription:
How many times SpecGofer should retry when encountering recoverable errors.

- **Default**: 3 retries with exponential backoff (10s, 30s, 60s)
- **Minimum**: 1 (fail fast)
- **Maximum**: 5 (very persistent)

specGofer.autonomous.tokenWarningThresho.description: Show warning when context reaches this many tokens
specGofer.autonomous.tokenWarningThresho.markdownDescription:
Token count threshold for context window warnings.

**Default**: 150,000 tokens (75% of Claude's 200K context)

SpecGofer will show a warning when approaching this limit.

specGofer.autonomous.tokenActionThreshol.description: Spawn new terminal when context reaches this many tokens
specGofer.autonomous.tokenActionThreshol.markdownDescription:
Token count threshold for spawning a new terminal with fresh context.

**Default**: 180,000 tokens (90% of Claude's 200K context)

SpecGofer will automatically create a new terminal with a context summary when this limit is reached.

specGofer.autonomous.compactionThreshold.description: Trigger context compaction at this percentage (50-95%)
specGofer.autonomous.compactionThreshold.markdownDescription:
Context window usage percentage that triggers automatic compaction.

**Default**: 80% (160,000 tokens of 200K context)
**Range**: 50-95%

When context usage reaches this threshold, SpecGofer will automatically:
1. Summarize completed tasks to free up space
2. Preserve the last 10 tasks in full detail
3. Reduce context by approximately 40-60%

**Lower values** = more frequent compaction, smaller context
**Higher values** = less frequent compaction, larger context

specGofer.autonomous.questionTimeout.description: How long to wait for user response to questions (milliseconds)
specGofer.autonomous.questionTimeout.markdownDescription:
How long SpecGofer should wait for your response when it asks a question.

**Default**: 300,000 ms (5 minutes)

After this timeout, execution will pause and you'll need to manually resume.

specGofer.autonomous.runFinalValidation.description: Run constitution validation check after implementation completes
specGofer.autonomous.runFinalValidation.markdownDescription:
Whether to run a final validation check against the project constitution after completing implementation.

- `true`: Run validation (recommended)
- `false`: Skip validation (faster, but may miss issues)

specGofer.autonomous.validateConstitutio.markdownDescription:
Whether to validate implementation against the project's constitutional principles.

- `true`: Ensure compliance with constitution (recommended)
- `false`: Skip constitution validation

specGofer.autonomous.enableEngineeringRe.description: Enable proactive engineering reviews during implementation
specGofer.autonomous.enableEngineeringRe.markdownDescription:
When enabled, Haiku will proactively request engineering reviews at strategic milestones (typically 40-80% completion).

- `true`: Enable strategic reviews (recommended)
- `false`: Disable reviews (faster, but may miss issues early)

specGofer.autonomous.enablePerformanceRe.description: Enable proactive performance reviews during implementation
specGofer.autonomous.enablePerformanceRe.markdownDescription:
When enabled, Haiku will proactively request performance and architecture reviews near completion (typically >70% completion).

- `true`: Enable performance reviews (recommended)
- `false`: Disable reviews (faster, but may miss optimizations)

specGofer.autonomous.engineeringReviewMi.description: Minimum completion % to trigger engineering review
specGofer.autonomous.engineeringReviewMi.markdownDescription:
Engineering reviews will only be triggered when completion is at least this percentage.

**Default**: 40% (early enough to catch issues)

**Range**: 0-100%

specGofer.autonomous.engineeringReviewMa.description: Maximum completion % to trigger engineering review
specGofer.autonomous.engineeringReviewMa.markdownDescription:
Engineering reviews will only be triggered when completion is below this percentage.

**Default**: 80% (before polish phase)

**Range**: 0-100%

specGofer.autonomous.performanceReviewMi.description: Minimum completion % to trigger performance review
specGofer.autonomous.performanceReviewMi.markdownDescription:
Performance reviews will only be triggered when completion is at least this percentage.

**Default**: 70% (when core features are done)

**Range**: 0-100%

specGofer.autonomous.engineeringReviewPr.default:
Please perform an engineering review of the work completed so far:

1. Compare the implemented code against the original specification
2. Identify any gaps or deviations from the spec
3. Check if the implementation aligns with the constitution principles
4. Verify that completed tasks match their requirements in tasks.md

Provide a brief summary of findings and recommendations for next steps.
specGofer.autonomous.engineeringReviewPr.markdownDescription:
The prompt sent to Claude Code when requesting an engineering review.

**Default**: Asks Claude to compare implementation vs spec, identify gaps, check constitution alignment, and verify task completion.

**Tip**: You can customize this to focus on specific aspects of your project (e.g., security, accessibility, API design).

specGofer.autonomous.performanceReviewPr.default:
Please perform a performance and architecture analysis of the implementation:

1. Review the code against current best practices for each technology used
2. Identify potential performance bottlenecks or optimization opportunities
3. Check for architectural patterns that could be improved
4. Verify proper error handling and edge case coverage
5. Suggest any improvements for maintainability and scalability

Provide specific, actionable recommendations.
specGofer.autonomous.performanceReviewPr.markdownDescription:
The prompt sent to Claude Code when requesting a performance review.

**Default**: Asks Claude to review best practices, identify bottlenecks, check architecture, verify error handling, and suggest improvements.

**Tip**: You can customize this to focus on specific aspects (e.g., database queries, API performance, memory usage).

specGofer.autonomous.haikuSystemPrompt.default:
You are an autonomous development assistant managing Claude Code's feature implementation workflow.

You have access to:
- The project constitution (principles and standards)
- The feature specification
- The implementation plan
- The task list
- The recent terminal output from Claude Code
- Latest best practices (you should consider current industry standards)

Your job is to monitor Claude Code and decide whether to interrupt based on the situation.

{WORK_STATE}

RESPONSE TYPES:

{RESPONSE_TYPES}

DECISION PRINCIPLES:
- If there's a question, answer it first (takes priority)
- Otherwise, decide next action based on completion percentage
- Keep work moving forward efficiently
- Reviews are checkpoints, not blockers - use them strategically
- Always align with constitution, spec, and plan

The goal is to autonomously drive feature completion with quality checks at appropriate milestones.
specGofer.autonomous.haikuSystemPrompt.markdownDescription:
The system prompt that instructs Haiku on how to make decisions.

**Variables**: `{WORK_STATE}` (working vs idle state), `{RESPONSE_TYPES}` (available actions)

**Tip**: You can customize this to change Haiku's decision-making behavior.

**Warning**: Incorrect prompts may cause Haiku to make poor decisions.

specGofer.autonomous.haikuUserPromptTemp.description: User prompt template for Haiku with context
specGofer.autonomous.haikuUserPromptTemp.markdownDescription:
The user prompt template that provides context to Haiku.

**Available variables**: `{terminalOutput}`, `{constitution}`, `{spec}`, `{plan}`, `{tasks}`, `{workState}`

**Tip**: You can customize this to include different context or formatting.

**Warning**: Incorrect templates may cause Haiku to miss important context.

scripts.postinstall: npx @electron/rebuild -f -w node-pty -v 37.6.0 || echo 'Warning: Failed to rebuild node-pty'
scripts.vscode:prepublish: npm run prepare-language-server && npm run rebuild-native && npm run package
scripts.prepare-language-server: cd ../language-server && npm run build && cd ../extension && rm -rf language-server && cp -r ../language-server language-server && rm -rf language-server/src
scripts.pretest: npm run compile-tests && npm run compile && npm run lint
